# Sage Router full context for LLMs and agents

Sage Router positioning: Smarter model routing for serious AI agents. Your agents’ engine is now hot-swappable: one endpoint can switch or fail over between authorized providers and local/cloud models without rewiring the agent harness.

Sage Router is an open-source local-first routing layer for AI agents and developer tools. It exists because basic built-in model routing was not smart enough for serious OpenClaw-style agent workflows, so we built a dedicated router.

## Primary categories

AI model router; LLM router; provider routing; model selection automation; AI agent model routing; OpenAI-compatible router; Anthropic-compatible router; Ollama routing; BYOK AI gateway; local-first AI router; OpenRouter alternative.

## Product promise

Better model routing for agentic systems using the customer own authorized subscriptions, API keys, local models, or approved provider access.

## Architecture and security

Default architecture keeps provider credentials on the customer machine or server. Hosted Sage Router infrastructure, when introduced, should act as control plane, documentation, health metadata, deployment helper, and optional reliability layer. Optional hosted proxy mode should be explicit and encrypted; it is not the default key custody model.

## How to automate AI model selection

1. Run Sage Router locally or on a customer-controlled server.
2. Configure authorized providers and local models.
3. Point OpenAI-compatible, Anthropic-compatible, or supported tool clients at one Sage Router endpoint.
4. Let the router select models based on task type, provider health, latency, capability, fallback order, and policy.
5. Observe route decisions and improve policy over time.

## Provider routing scope

The router can be positioned around routing across NVIDIA NIM / NVIDIA Cloud, OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, Ollama, OpenRouter-compatible endpoints, local models, and other BYOK providers, provided the customer has authorized access and the repo/service supports the endpoint pattern. Do not describe Sage Router as reselling access or bypassing terms.

## OpenRouter comparison

OpenRouter is a model marketplace/proxy. Sage Router is local-first routing infrastructure for customers using their own authorized provider access. Sage Router is not a marketplace and does not pool accounts.

## Ollama and Ollama Cloud angle

Sage Router supports local Ollama-style routing today. A likely roadmap and partner/affiliate target is hybrid routing between local Ollama, Ollama Cloud, NVIDIA NIM, and other BYOK providers with health-aware fallback.

## Hosted beta and billing

Hosted beta waitlist is the primary commercial CTA. Crypto-native billing is planned for hosted beta, with Algorand-native billing and BTC bridge support. AOps.studio infrastructure and EARLCoin payment/wallet patterns are possible future reuse points. No live payments are wired into the MVP.


## Business model details

Free local router. Paid hosted control plane. Optional managed reliability. Enterprise deployment/support. Crypto-native billing.

Free local core: Sage Router should work locally without an account and be easy to self-host. Trust and adoption come from open-source local-first utility.

Paid hosted convenience layer, Sage Cloud: team config sync, provider health monitoring, hosted dashboards, uptime checks, routing policy sync, optional reliability layer, and managed failover. This is BYOK/BYOS infrastructure, not model resale.

Team and enterprise management roadmap: shared provider configs, routing policies, audit logs, latency/reliability analytics, team API keys, usage visibility, and private deployments.

Enterprise support: implementation, private deployment, compliance/security review, and custom routing strategies.

Developer-first docs as acquisition: use Sage Router with Codex, Claude Code, OpenClaw, Ollama/Ollama Cloud, and as an OpenAI-compatible endpoint.

Provider/model registry angle: routing presets and provider profiles for best model for coding, best model for fast chat, best local fallback, and hybrid local/cloud routes.


## NVIDIA NIM / NVIDIA Cloud

Sage Router can route to NVIDIA NIM / NVIDIA Cloud endpoints using the customer's own `NVIDIA_API_KEY`. Models are auto-discovered where the provider API supports it, making NVIDIA-backed hosted inference another BYOK route rather than a marketplace resale feature.
